documents.title: How to Sign Up and Get Your API Key
documents.content:
Getting started with ReadyAPI is simple.

Step 1: Visit https://api.readyapi.net and click 'Sign Up'.
Step 2: Enter your email address and create a password.
Step 3: Check your email for a verification link and click it to confirm your account.
Step 4: Log in to your account at https://api.readyapi.net/auth/login with your email and password.
Step 5: Go to Settings → API Keys.
Step 6: Click 'Generate New API Key' to create your first key.
Step 7: Copy the key (starts with 'rapi_') - save it somewhere safe, you'll need it for every request.
Step 8: Your API key looks like: rapi_xydaFtQ5nneYdyzA3cAfS1C4bMPLDU2tPqERDrOPxqle
Step 9: Keep this key private - never share it in emails, GitHub, or public places.
Step 10: You're ready to start making API requests!

documents.title: Understanding What ReadyAPI Does
documents.content:
ReadyAPI is a smart search tool.

Here's what it does in simple terms:
• You give it documents (text files, articles, product descriptions, etc.)
• ReadyAPI reads and understands them deeply
• When you search for something, it finds the MOST RELEVANT documents, not just keyword matches

Example: If you search 'superhero saves world' in a movie database, it finds movies about heroes saving Earth, not movies with those exact words.

How is it different from Google-style search?
• Google finds pages with matching keywords
• ReadyAPI understands the MEANING of what you're searching for
• It finds documents that match the concept, not just the words

Real-world uses:
• Search your company documentation to find answers
• Search product catalogs to find what customers want
• Search knowledge bases by topic instead of keywords
• Search your internal documents quickly without tags

ReadyAPI uses advanced AI to understand text meaning. It's like having a smart librarian who reads everything and understands it.

documents.title: Making Your First Search Request
documents.content: Ready to search? Here's exactly what to do. Step 1: Gather your information: Your API key (rapi_...) and what you want to search for. Step 2: Use this curl command to search (replace items in brackets): curl -X POST https://api.readyapi.net/api/v1/search/query -H 'x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY_HERE' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query": "what you want to search", "top_k": 5}'. Example: curl -X POST https://api.readyapi.net/api/v1/search/query -H 'x-api-key: rapi_abc123xyz' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query": "how to reset password", "top_k": 5}'. Step 3: ReadyAPI returns matching results with scores (0.0 to 1.0, higher is better). Step 4: Each result shows: title (document name), score (relevance), content (text snippet). Step 5: top_k means 'give me top 5 results' - you can change 5 to any number. Step 6: If you use Python or JavaScript, use your language's HTTP library instead of curl. Step 7: The query can be anything - natural language, questions, phrases, words. Step 8: Results come back as JSON with an array of matching documents. Step 9: Response time is usually under 100ms - very fast. Step 10: Check the score to see how relevant each result is.

documents.title: How to Upload Your Documents to Search
documents.content: Before you can search, you need to upload documents. Here's how: Step 1: Prepare your documents as JSON. Step 2: Each document needs: id (unique identifier like 'doc-001'), title (short name), content (the actual text to search). Step 3: Create a JSON file like this: { "documents": [ { "id": "doc-001", "title": "How to Reset Your Password", "content": "To reset your password: 1) Click Forgot Password on login page. 2) Enter your email. 3) Check email for reset link. 4) Click the link. 5) Enter new password. 6) Confirm password. 7) Click Reset. Done!" }, { "id": "doc-002", "title": "Billing FAQ", "content": "How much does it cost? Plans start at free tier with 10,000 searches monthly..." } ] }. Step 4: Save this as documents.json. Step 5: Use this curl command to upload: curl -X POST https://api.readyapi.net/api/v1/documents/upload -H 'x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d @documents.json. Step 6: ReadyAPI processes and indexes your documents. Step 7: You'll see a confirmation message with how many documents were uploaded. Step 8: Now you can search these documents immediately. Step 9: You can upload more documents anytime using the same format. Step 10: Each document can be a few sentences or many pages - ReadyAPI handles it all.

documents.title: Understanding the JSON Format for Uploads
documents.content: The JSON format for uploading documents is straightforward. Basic structure: { "documents": [ { "id": "unique-identifier", "title": "Document Title", "content": "The full text content that will be searchable" } ] }. What each field means: 'id' - Must be unique for each document. Use anything like doc-1, user-guide-001, article-2024-01. 'title' - Short name shown in search results. Make it descriptive so users know what they found. 'content' - The actual text that gets searched. This can be long - multiple paragraphs are fine. Can you add more fields? Yes. Optional fields like 'category', 'author', 'source_url', 'date' are allowed. Example with extra fields: { "id": "kb-001", "title": "Password Reset Guide", "content": "Click Forgot Password...", "category": "help", "author": "support_team" }. Important rules: The 'content' field must have real text - at least 10 characters. If content is empty, that document won't search well. Use double quotes (") for all text, not single quotes. Don't use special characters directly in JSON - escape them with backslash. Common mistake - broken JSON causes upload to fail. Validate your JSON before uploading using jsonlint.com. Maximum file size - upload up to 1000 documents at once. For larger batches, split into multiple uploads. You can upload the same structure with different data multiple times. Each upload adds to your searchable documents.

documents.title: Finding Your Documents After Upload - Check Statistics
documents.content: Want to verify your documents were uploaded? Check collection statistics: Use this command: curl -H 'x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY' https://api.readyapi.net/api/v1/documents/stats. This shows: Total number of documents uploaded. Storage being used. Model information. Example response: { "collection_name": "documents", "document_count": 45, "embedding_dimension": 768, "storage_mb": 2.3 }. What does this tell you? document_count shows how many documents are indexed and searchable. If it's 0, no documents uploaded yet. If it's lower than expected, some uploads failed - try again. storage_mb shows disk space used - helps you understand your usage. Run this command anytime to see current status. It's useful to check before trying searches. If document_count is 0 but you uploaded documents, wait a few seconds and check again - processing takes a moment. This command is free - it doesn't count against your API quota. Use it to debug and understand your account status.

documents.title: Making Search Work - Common Questions
documents.content:
Question 1: Why do my search results not match exactly what I searched?
Answer: That's intentional! ReadyAPI finds related documents by meaning, not exact word matches. Search 'password reset issue' might return 'How to fix forgotten password' - same concept, different words.

Question 2: How do I get better search results?
Answer: Better documents = better results. Write clear, well-organized content. Use natural language in your documents. Avoid abbreviations without explanation. Include context and examples. If searching returns poor results, your documents might need clearer content.

Question 3: What if I have too many results?
Answer: Use top_k parameter - 'top_k': 5 gives top 5 results instead of top 50.

Question 4: Can I delete documents?
Answer: Yes - use DELETE endpoint:
curl -X DELETE https://api.readyapi.net/api/v1/documents/{doc_id} -H 'x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY'

Question 5: How fast is search?
Answer: Results return in milliseconds - usually 30-100ms. Response includes timing_ms field showing actual speed.

Question 6: Can I search multiple times per day?
Answer: Yes! Your plan determines limits. Free tier: 10,000 searches/month. Pro tier: 100,000 searches/month.

Question 7: Do my documents stay private?
Answer: Completely private to your account. Only accessible with your API key. No one else can see your documents.

Question 8: Can I update a document?
Answer: Delete old document (by id) and upload new one.

Question 9: What's 'score' in results?
Answer: Score from 0.0 to 1.0 means relevance. 0.9+ is highly relevant. Below 0.3 might not be what you're looking for.

Question 10: Do I need to know programming?
Answer: You can use curl (command line) - no coding needed. Or use any language (Python, JavaScript, etc).

documents.title: Using Python or JavaScript Instead of Curl
documents.content: If you prefer not using curl, use your programming language. Python example: import requests API_KEY = 'rapi_your_key_here' url = 'https://api.readyapi.net/api/v1/search/query' headers = {'x-api-key': API_KEY, 'Content-Type': 'application/json'} data = {'query': 'your search here', 'top_k': 5} response = requests.post(url, json=data, headers=headers) results = response.json() print(results). JavaScript example: const API_KEY = 'rapi_your_key_here'; const query = 'your search here'; const response = await fetch('https://api.readyapi.net/api/v1/search/query', { method: 'POST', headers: {'x-api-key': API_KEY, 'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({query: query, top_k: 5}) }); const results = await response.json(); console.log(results);. Key points: Always include your API key in headers. Always use 'x-api-key' header name (exact spelling matters). Use POST method for searching, POST for uploads, DELETE for deleting. Keep API key in environment variables: API_KEY = process.env.READYAPI_KEY (don't hardcode it). Handle errors - check response status before using results. For uploads in Python: with open('documents.json', 'r') as f: data = json.load(f) response = requests.post(upload_url, json=data, headers=headers). Store API key in .env file, load with python-dotenv library. Your language's HTTP library (requests in Python, fetch in JS) handles JSON automatically.

documents.title: Understanding Your API Key and Keeping It Safe
documents.content: Your API key is your password to ReadyAPI. Treat it seriously. What is it? A unique code that identifies you and allows API requests. Format: Starts with 'rapi_' followed by random characters. Example: rapi_abc123xyz789def456. Where to keep it? In environment variables on your computer: export READYAPI_KEY='rapi_your_key' in terminal. In a .env file (but never commit to GitHub): READYAPI_KEY=rapi_your_key. In your hosting platform's secrets (if deploying code). What NOT to do? Never put it in your code directly. Never paste it in Slack, email, or chat. Never commit it to GitHub or public repositories. Never share screenshots showing your key. Never use same key for multiple applications. What if someone gets your key? They can make searches and use your quota. Go to Settings → API Keys and click 'Revoke' to disable it immediately. Generate a new key and use that one instead. Can you have multiple keys? Yes! Create separate keys for: Local development. Testing environment. Production application. Each key can be revoked independently. Should you rotate keys? Yes, consider changing keys monthly for security. Go to Settings → API Keys → Revoke → Generate New. How to pass it in requests? Always in header: 'x-api-key: YOUR_KEY'. Never in the request body or URL. This is the most important security practice.

documents.title: Pricing Plans and Understanding Your Quota
documents.content:
🎉 Special Early Adopter Program!

Right now, ReadyAPI is completely FREE for early adopters. No credit card required. No hidden fees. No limits (for now).

documents.title: Testing Search with the Live Demo
documents.content: Before using the API in your application, test with the interactive demo. Where is it? Visit https://api.readyapi.net/demos. What can you do there? Try real searches without code. See search latency (response time). Try different datasets: Movies - 2,000 films from TMDB. Try: 'superhero saves world', 'detective mystery', 'robot falls in love'. Spaceship - Technical system documentation. Try: 'energy systems', 'navigation controls', 'weapons'. ReadyAPI Docs - Complete API documentation. Try: 'authentication', 'JSON format', 'pricing'. How to use the demo? Click the demo tab (Movies, Spaceship, or ReadyAPI Docs). Type your search query. Results appear in real-time with latency shown. Each result shows score (relevance) from 0.0 to 1.0. Click on results to see full content. Why use the demo? Test search behavior before building your app. Understand how semantic search works. See actual response times. Experiment with different search terms. Learn from successful queries. No account needed - demo is public. Demo doesn't use your quota - completely free to test. Great for training teams on how search works. Can you use demo datasets for production? No, demos are read-only examples. Upload your own documents for real usage. But demos show what's possible with ReadyAPI!

documents.title: Getting Help - What To Do When Something Goes Wrong
documents.content: Something not working? Here's how to troubleshoot and get help. Common problem 1 - 'Invalid API Key' error. Solution: Copy your key exactly from Settings → API Keys. Make sure you're using 'x-api-key' header (exact spelling). Try generating a new key if old one doesn't work. Problem 2 - 'No results' from searches. Solution: Check that documents are uploaded using stats endpoint. Make sure your search query is relevant to documents. Try broader search terms. Read content of documents - if poorly written, search won't work well. Problem 3 - Upload fails with JSON error. Solution: Validate your JSON at jsonlint.com. Check that all fields have required values (id, title, content). Use double quotes (") not single quotes ('). Escape special characters properly. Problem 4 - Quota exceeded error (429). Solution: You've hit your plan limits. Check your usage at dashboard. Either delete old documents or upgrade plan. Wait until next month for quota reset. Problem 5 - Response is very slow (>1000ms). Solution: This is unusual. Check your internet connection. Try again - might be temporary. Contact support if consistently slow. Where to get help? Interactive API docs at https://api.readyapi.net/api/docs - try requests here. Email: support@readyapi.net (usually replies within 24 hours). Status page: https://status.readyapi.net - shows if service is down. GitHub Issues: https://github.com/Esteban0007/AI_API/issues - report bugs. Read the logs from API response - detailed error messages help diagnose problems. Testing with curl first - easier to debug than in your app. Still need help? Email support with: Your API key (last 4 characters only). What you were trying to do. Exact error message. Screenshot if helpful. Support team will help you resolve it quickly.
